title: Omer Avital -- Jazz in Duketown
subTitle: Israeli-American double bassist Omer Avital plays radiantly energetic, resounding jazz with oriental influences; at Jazz in Duketown 2019, Avital presents singular repertoire based on his album "Qantar."

title: Aretha Franklin in Paris
subTitle: Recording at the Palais Des Sports from 1977; performing songs like "Respect," "You Make Me Feel Like a Natural Woman," "La Vie en Rose" and "Singing in the Rain."

title: Live in Australia - Louis Armstrong, Billy Kyle, Trummy Young & Aervell Shaw
subTitle: In 1964 Louis Armstrong recorded his biggest-selling record, Hello, Dolly! He made assorted television appearances, especially in the 1950s and 1960s - the recorded film was a TV Show in Australia when Armstrong was at the peak of his career.

title: Lady Day: The Many Faces of Billie Holiday
subTitle: Film clips show the jazz/blues artist performing "God Bless the Child" and "Please Don't Talk About Me When I'm Gone."

title: Mack the knife
subTitle: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential male jazz and pop vocalists in history showcases these legends' unique essence.

title: Estival Jazz Lugano
subTitle: In 1996, renowned American saxophonist Wayne Shorter and his quintet perform at Estival Jazz Lugano; together with pianist Jim Beard, guitarist David Gilmore, bassist Alphonso Johnson and drummer Rodney van Holmes.

title: Vigil
subTitle: Explore the music of an influential and innovative saxophonist in jazz: John Coltrane; get immersed in this collection of performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's artistry.

title: John Lewis Piano Solo
subTitle: John Lewis Piano Solo

title: Miles Davis: Jazz Greats
subTitle: American trumpeter Miles Davis pioneered bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music; on October 11, 1964, he performed in Milan with tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams.

title: Raul Midón at Sing Jazz, Singapore 2017
subTitle: Raul Midón appeared at 2017's Sing Jazz Festival in Singapore with double bassist Romeir Mendez and drummer Billy Williams.

title: Münchner Klaviersommer: Eliane Elias Trio
subTitle: In this live concert at the Philharmonie in Munich, Eliane Elias shows her distinctive and immediately recognizable musical style, which blends her Brazilian samba roots and voice with her instrumental bebop, jazz and compositional skills.

title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

subTitle: Two concerts from Schloss Ansbach in 1978: one by Gil Evans and another by Ornette Coleman.

subTitle: Raul Midón appears at 2017's Sing Jazz Festival in Singapore with double bassist Romeir Mendez and drummer Billy Williams.

title: Live at Estival Lugano 1986 -- Carla Bley, Steve Swallow, Wayne Krantz & Victor Lewis
subTitle: Estival offers a line-up that explores the world of contemporary music; discover the bond between pianist Carla Bley and bassist Steve Swallow.

title: November Music - Tigran Hamasyan & Mathias Eick
subTitle: November Music, a Dutch music festival, showcases prominent composers, ensembles and pioneers of jazz and world music; Armenian pianist Hamasyan; Norwegian trumpeter Mathias Eick.

subTitle: Bill Evans: Live in '64, '65 & '70 features five separate performances by one of the most revered pianists in music history.

subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosts the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Walter Trout, who was born in New Jersey and is based in Los Angeles, is a world-famous contemporary blues rock guitarist and vocalist; the Walter Trout Band performs at the 50th Burghausen International Jazz Week in 2019.

subTitle: Avishai Cohen's trio with Noam David and Omri Mor join forces with the International Symphony Orchestra INSO-Lviv, conducted by Christian Schumann.

title: Miriam Makeba Live at Estival Lugano 1985
subTitle: Miriam Makeba, also named Mama Africa, was a South African singer, actress, United Nations goodwill ambassador, and civil-rights activist; she brought African music to a Western audience, and popularized the world music and Afropop genres.

title: Quincy Jones: 'Q' conducting his 'Dream Band'
subTitle: Jazz Icons: Quincy Jones'spotlights a young 'Q' conducting his 'dream band'-an 18-piece orchestra of world-renowned players such as Clark Terry, Phil Woods, Sahib Shihab, Budd Johnson and Benny Bailey. This recording features 5 songs and is one of the finest examples of big band jazz ever to be captured on film. Shot in Belgium in 1961, this concert is one of the only known visual documents of this legendary tour.
